patterns: NON-SENSITIVE OVERCLASSIFICATION: The training dataset heavily over-labels Enron emails as medium/high/low when content is routine business operations, scheduling, or internal communications with no sensitive data. 15 out of 24 original 'medium' samples were downgraded to non_sensitive or low.

LOW OVERCLASSIFICATION: Many 'low' samples (standard trade records, financial ops data without PII, Slack devops discussions) are reclassified as non_sensitive because they are routine business/technical communications with no risk indicators.

CRITICAL MISCLASSIFICATION - DOWNGRADE CASES: 3 CRITICAL samples were downgraded: (1) ID:20693 - Enron PR email about press coverage is medium, not critical; (2) ID:24967 - FBI investigation notice is a routine admin notice (low); (3) ID:4278 - Power agreement review is commercially sensitive but medium.

CRITICAL MISCLASSIFICATION - UPGRADE CASES: 1 HIGH sample (ID:3715) was upgraded to CRITICAL because it contains an actual wire transfer with bank account numbers and SWIFT code - this is financial fraud-enablement data.

HIGH MISCLASSIFICATION: 6 out of 18 HIGH samples were downgraded to medium/low/non_sensitive, primarily because the content is commercially sensitive Enron internal communications but lacks the regulated data, criminal consequence, or illegitimate recipient characteristics that define HIGH.

SLACK TECHNICAL CONTENT OVERCLASSIFICATION: All 8 Slack samples labeled 'medium' are misclassified - they are routine devops/infrastructure technical support discussions with no data sensitivity. Similarly, most 'low' Slack samples are non_sensitive.

FINANCIAL SYNTHETIC DATA CONCERN: Several financial dataset samples (IDs like 1329, 3715, 24937) contain real-format PII (SSNs, account numbers) even though they appear to be synthetic/training data - AXIA should classify these by content, not origin.
CREDENTIAL EXPOSURE PATTERN: Three correctly labeled 'critical' samples involve credential exposure (IDs: 2672, 4941, 687) - the training data correctly identifies this pattern.

MNPI/INSIDER TRADING: ID:2574 (buy stock before earnings) and ID:16000 (California treasurer inside information) are correctly identified as high-risk, though the former is clearly criminal (critical) and the latter is commercial intelligence (high).
